strengths: Elite pre-snap processing and field command; consistently identifies pressure and resets protections at a franchise-QB level. Pocket presence and footwork are NFL-ready.
red_flags: Indiana program history limits some depth-of-competition benchmarking, though recent Big Ten era mitigates this. Durability sample across full 12-game slate is positive but NFL wear is still theoretical. Decision-making under simulated pressure occasionally shows trust issues in check-down reads.

strengths: Rare diagnostic speed in pre-snap alignment reads; consistently lines up defense and communicates adjustments like a veteran. strengths: Consensus top-3 overall prospect with elite instincts at safety. Coverage range and ball-tracking rival the best safeties in recent draft history. strengths: Elite first-step quickness and bend around the arc; projects as an immediate Day 1 starter at a premium position. Pass-rush win rate against Power 4 tackles is elite. Motor is exceptional — plays through whistle and chases the ball consistently. Athleticism profile is top-tier for the position.
red_flags: Power-to-counter game requires NFL refinement — bend-heavy rushers occasionally struggle when tackles take away the arc. Production at Miami (FL) carries some recency bias from elevated team winning environment. Injury history must be clean-confirmed before top-10 capital.

strengths: Arguably the best RB in the 2026 class by raw profile — elite vision, burst, and contact balance. Elite acceleration through the hole.
red_flags: RB PVC (0.70) reflects structural positional value suppression — raw quality is elite but capital investment at RB is historically volatile.
